design_philosophy.core_loop: Switch between four characters with distinct abilities to navigate platforming challenges and defeat bosses; each character excels in different scenarios (climbing, flying, strength, speed/access)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Character selection strategy—choosing the right hero for each obstacle or boss encounter, managing shared lives across four separate characters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a team of diverse heroes (climber, dragon, golem, mouse) to defeat the demon king Ta-Keed and save the kingdom of Forgy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mandatory character-swapping mechanic that ties strategy directly to platforming design; Mega Man-inspired stage select with character assignment (each hero gets a tutorial stage); permanent character death until stage completion unless a potion revives them; only Samson's death triggers full restart, creating asymmetric consequence design

difficulty.lives_system: Shared pool of lives across all four characters; losing Samson forces restart of stage; other characters can remain dead until stage completion or revived by potion
difficulty.checkpointing: Password system allows resume after boss defeat; no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Two difficulty levels selectable (Easy and Normal); Normal difficulty unlocks true final boss; player chooses character order for first four tutorial stages; strategic character selection determines how to approach obstacles

progression.upgrade_system: Character-intrinsic abilities only; no persistent upgrades. Per-stage collectibles: extra lives, health-replenishing hearts, crystal orbs (increase max health capacity), single-potion-per-character recovery items
progression.unlock_mechanism: First four stages act as character tutorials and can be played in any order; completing all four stages advances to linear sequence of remaining stages; Normal difficulty unlocks true final boss (Easy difficulty has alternate final encounter)
replayability_hooks: Two difficulty levels with different final bosses
Twenty stages with branching paths within each
Character selection strategy varies optimal approach per stage
Collecting crystal orbs to permanently increase character health capacity encourages replay
Password system enables targeted stage practice

world_building_techniques: Story setup: demon king Ta-Keed sealed by gods, his power dispersed as four bells; Ta-Keed released by lightning and threatens kingdom
Character introduction via stage progression: each hero summoned by Emperor Hans XIV
Character hierarchy established through narrative: Hans XIV appoints Samson as leader; Kikira challenges him in a boss fight to determine dominance
Regional narrative differences: Japanese version goes directly to intro cutscene on startup; international versions show title screen first
Staff roll displays narrative conclusion: international version shows heroes being congratulated by crowd of soldiers with mountainscape; Japanese version uses black background (more austere)

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in a boss encounter; bosses appear designed to leverage specific character strengths or require character-switching strategy; at least one boss (knight in palace stage) summons environmental hazards (lightning)
notable_bosses: Knight boss (palace stage): summons lightning attacks
Kikira (character vs. character encounter): fought after completing four tutorial stages; players defeat her to establish Samson's leadership

faction_indicators: Kingdom of Forgy vs. demon king Ta-Keed
Emperor Hans XIV and his army vs. Ta-Keed's subordinates
Four summoned heroes as unified team vs. Ta-Keed's forces
danger_signals: Ta-Keed's lightning release from ancient seal
Emperor's army powerless against demon king's subordinates
Multiple boss encounters per stage suggest escalating threat
environmental_storytelling: Palace setting with knight bosses indicates civilization under siege
Multiple stage environments (implied: wizard stage with griffin statue) show ta-Keed's corruption spreading across varied terrain
Character abilities tied to archetypal roles: climber, dragon, golem, mouse suggest diverse coalition of unlikely heroes

sources_used: tcrf: Little Samson - The Cutting Room Floor
